title: Taris dueling ring

{{Quote|Ladies and gentlemen, I draw your attention to the dueling ring. Here, two combatants will battle for your viewing and gambling enjoyment. Now, I hope all your bets are down, because we're ready to roll!|Dueling ring announcer|<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic" />}}
The '''Taris dueling ring''' was an arena that featured sanctioned [[Stun duel|duels]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Taris/Legends|Taris]]. Travelers and residents alike were permitted to fight using weapons of choice to earn [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]], as well as fame. The ring was a profitable business venture in the [[Time/Legends|years]] leading up to and during the [[Jedi Civil War]], offering spectating and [[Gambling/Legends|gambling]] entertainment. During this time, it was owned by [[Ajuur]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]], and featured such famous duelists as [[Gorse Bendak|Bendak Starkiller]], [[Ice (duelist)|Ice]], [[Twitch (Rodian)|Twitch]], and [[Revan/Legends|Revan]]. Tickets to watch the matches were sold to patrons and gambling on the outcome provided a means of rewarding the winning duelists and grossing additional profit for management. However, in [[3956 BBY]], most of Taris was [[Destruction of Taris|destroyed]] by [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|orbital bombardment]]. Nevertheless, an arena was active on Taris during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] after the [[Upper City]] had been rebuilt.

==Description==
Located in the [[Upper City Cantina|cantina]] of the [[Taris/Legends|Tarisian]] [[Upper City]], the Taris [[Stun duel|dueling]] ring was a battle arena where inhabitants of the [[Ecumenopolis/Legends|ecumenopolis]] of Taris could duel to earn [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]]. The dueling ring was owned and fully-operated by the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Ajuur]], who had ties with [[Davik Kang]], a prominent member of the [[Exchange]] crime syndicate. Ajuur personally arranged the fights and often sought match-ups that would be of highest interest to potential spectators. Even though it was sustained through its entertainment value, dueling was considered a viable sport by duelists.<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic" />

===Duels===
[[File:Gerlon-defeat-deadeye-duel.jpg|thumb|left|225px|A defeat of [[Deadeye Duncan]] in the duel ring by [[Gerlon Two-Fingers]]]]
Each match only consisted of two duelists who sparred until one was rendered unconscious. Each duelist was allowed to battle with any weapon of choice. Some competitors fought with [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]], [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifles]] and even [[Grenade/Legends|grenades]]. Others, such as [[Marl]], preferred [[Vibroblade/Legends|vibroblades]] and [[Vibrosword/Legends|vibroswords]]. [[Armor/Legends|Armor]] was permitted to be worn by duelists, and it was permissible for other aids, such as [[Medpac/Legends|medpacs]] and [[Stimulant/Legends|stimulants]], to be used as well. In spite of fighting with deadly weapons, there was an extremely low chance of [[Death/Legends|death]]. [[Energy suppression field]]s covered the arena, specifically the ring itself. While the weapons still caused a measure of pain, the fields allowed for the competitors to only be knocked unconscious rather than being killed. In the event of serious injuries, [[Medical droid/Legends|medical droids]] were available on site. On the other hand, sanctioned death matches had been permitted in the past. These matches were eventually banned. It was still enforced by the [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith]] when they began their [[Capture of Taris|occupation of Taris]].<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic" />

===Spectating===
[[File:Taris_dueling_ring_spect_area.png|thumb|right|225px|The spectator's area outside the arena ring]]
Inside the arena venue, spectators who purchased tickets watched the matches in-person. Tickets were purchasable in the cantina area through Ajuur. Since seating was limited, not all spectators were sold tickets and allowed into the venue. In the cantina area adjacent to the arena, [[Viewscreen/Legends|viewscreens]] were set up for additional interested spectators to watch the fights free of charge if they did not have tickets. Monitors were also set up in other locations of the cantina. [[Gambling/Legends|Gambling]] on the duels was permitted, if not encouraged, and provided significant business. All wagers were placed through Ajuur, who managed the gambling on the fights.<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic" />

===Winnings===
The actual amount of credits a winner earned for defeating a competitor varied. Under Ajuur's ownership, the standard prize for winning a match was 10% of the profit from all the wagers. Winning duelists were paid directly, and losing duelists earned no reward. Since the reward was linked to gambling, a match that garnered more interest and wagers was more profitable for the winner.<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic" />

==History==
{{GameLSmechanics}}
The Taris dueling ring was noted to have existed since the years following the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]. Over [[Time/Legends|time]] it began to feature such prominent fighters as [[Gorse Bendak|Bendak Starkiller]], who boasted to have never lost a death match. Once death matches were outlawed, Starkiller went into retirement. During the [[Jedi Civil War]], the number of duelists dropped. Following the destruction of the ''[[Endar Spire]]'', Taris was blockaded by [[Darth Malak]]'s ships, preventing all interplanetary travel. The dueling ring, like other establishments on Taris, suffered from this, as potential competitors were prevented from reaching the planet. There were still, however, a few remaining duelists actively competing. In [[3956 BBY]], when [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] and [[Carth Onasi]] crash-landed on Taris, Revan fought as a duelist by competing under the pseudonym ''[[Mysterious Stranger]]''. The ''Mysterious Stranger'' defeated all competing duelists Taris had to offer including [[Marl]], [[Ice (duelist)|Ice]] and the [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Twitch (Rodian)|Twitch]], the reigning champion. By becoming the new champion, Revan earned a reputation that even extended into the [[Lower City]].<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic" />
{{Endgame}}

While Revan was fleeing Taris with his party, the Sith [[Destruction of Taris|decimated the planet]] by [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|orbital bombardment]]. Carth Onasi once spoke of Taris, noting that "no building over two stories was left standing".<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic" /> Large portions of the Upper City were rebuilt by [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], and a dueling ring was once again active in the rebuilt city. The ring featured a [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] by the name of [[Twitch (Gungan)|Twitch]] as its champion.<ref name="Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption">''[[Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The Taris dueling ring first appeared in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''. The player, as Revan, has the choice of competing in the dueling ring, though it is not required to successfully finish the game. Once the player has defeated all active duelists, they have the choice of battling the retired Bendak Starkiller in an illegal death match. However, accepting Bendak's challenge leads to the player gaining [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] points, and Revan is, [[canon]]ically, [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light sided]].<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic" />
